name: Nepal
description: Nepal is the trekking capital of the world, home to eight of the ten highest peaks on Earth including Mount Everest. From the subtropical jungles of Chitwan to the icy heights of the Khumbu, Nepal offers an unmatched diversity of landscapes, cultures, and adventure.

name: Bhutan
description: Bhutan is the world's only carbon-negative country and measures its prosperity by Gross National Happiness. This pristine Himalayan kingdom offers ancient monasteries, colourful festivals, and untouched landscapes — all within a strictly managed, high-value tourism model.

name: Tibet
description: Tibet, the "Roof of the World", is a land of vast plateaus, ancient Buddhist monasteries, and breathtaking mountain scenery. The sacred city of Lhasa, the Potala Palace, and the remote Everest North Base Camp make it one of the most spiritually and visually compelling destinations on Earth.

name: India
description: India's Himalayan states offer some of the most varied trekking and adventure experiences in Asia. From the rhododendron-clad slopes of Sikkim to the remote valleys of Ladakh and Spiti, the subcontinent's northern reaches are a world unto themselves.

name: Peru
description: Peru combines two of the world's great wildernesses: the high Andes and the Amazon rainforest. The legendary Inca Trail to Machu Picchu is one of the most iconic treks on Earth, while the Ausangate and Huayhuash circuits challenge even experienced mountaineers.
